titleStmt.title: SII 28.278: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 278. (A.R. No. 432 of 1913.) ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.

summary.p: Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, on the north base of the maṇḍapa infront.

p.hi: Jaṭāvarmaṉ Tribhuvanachakravartti Sundarapāṇḍya Year 13, Mithuna, śu. 13, Friday. Anusham^1^

summary.p: It is stated that sometime before, there was a disagreement between the vāṇiga-nagarattār and cheṭṭis, whereby the former humiliated the latter, in respect of the conduct of the festivals in the months of Āṉi, Mārgaḻi and Māśi for God Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār, the God (uḍaiyār) of Āṟagaḻūr in Āṟṟūrkūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jaṉaṉātha-vaḷanāḍu. Apparently as a consequence of the above dispute the temple sold it as chaṇḍēśvara-pperuvilai to the former body the privilege of providing the various items required (listed) for conducting the stated festivals which had suffered. However, in due course, the festivals suffered (due to the nonreceipt of the required items from those who had taken over the responsibility to supply them), seeing this, the nāṭṭavar of Āṟṟūr intervened and granted (the village ?) Talaivāy in Āṟṟūr-nāḍu as iṟai-ili (tax-free grant) with exemption from all āyams (levies) including poṉ-vari as also nāṭṭu-viṉiyōgam (contributions towards the expenses of the nāḍu) to enable the conduct of the stated festivals without any short-coming or interruptions. However it was stipulated that the kaḍamai which was outside the jurisdiction (kāṇi-ppaṟṟu), Āṟṟūr-nāḍu might be collected (from the granted village).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . koccaṭapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tevarkku yāṇṭu patiṉmuṉṟāvatu mituṉa nāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu trayodaśiyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa Aniḻattu nāḷThese details lend themselves to equated to the following dates in the ñulian calendar 1) 1262 A.D., ñune 2 (·20; ·57); 2) 1265 A.D., May 29 (·28; ·96); 3) 1282 A.D., ñune 19 (f.d.t. ·52); ·49)• 44) 1285 A.D., ñune 15 (f.d.t. ·58; ·93); 5) 1289 A.D., ñune 3 (·43; ·71); 6) 1292 A.D., May 30 (·38; ·97) and 7) 1316 A.D., ñune 4 (·64; ·83). Hence it is not possible to attribute the record definitely to the reign period of anyone of the four (as per ḷ.D.S. Pillai, king with the name as cited above) (contra A.ṟ.Ep., 1914 p. 64).malāṭāṉa jaṉaṉāta vaḷanāṭṭu Āṟṟūr kūṟṟattu Āṟakaḷūruṭaiyār tirukkāmīśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku tiruvāṉit tirunāḷukkum tirumārkaḻit tirunāḷukkum tirumācit
tirunāḷukkum muṉṉāḷ vāṇika nakarattār ceṭṭikaḷuṭaṉ piṇaṅki Ivarkaḷait tāḻvu kāṭṭi nāṅkaḷ Ittiruppaṇi Eṟiṭṭukkoḷḷa Eṉṟa Aḷavil niṅkaḷ caṇṭeśvarap peruvilaiyākak koṇṭu tirunāḷpaṭitevai Uḷḷatu kuṟaivaṟukkiṟom Eṉkaiyil Ivarkaḷ Ittiruccāntu cātti Eḻuntaruḷippuṟappaṭumpaṭikku tirumeṟpūccu tirupparivaṭṭam tirumālai tirukkāppu nāṇ tiruppāvāṭai
kkuṟaivaṟuppu tiruviṉaikkuṭai tiruvālavaṭṭam tirukkoṭi Āṭai caṭṭattukku pili Uḷḷiṭṭavai pantam Eṇṇai taṭṭuppaṭāmal ceyvikka kaṭavatāka cimāheśvarakaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukku muṟikuṭuttu Ivarkaḷ caṇṭeśvarap peruvilai koṇṭu Uṭaiyatāṉa Ittiruppaṇi tāḻvu paṭukaiyāley Āṟṟūr nāṭṭavar kaṇṭu Ittāḻvu keṭṭu Ippaṭi tāḻvu paṭalākāteṉṟum Āṟṟūr nāṭṭukku Āṟakaḷūruṭaiyār
tirukkāmiśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirunāḷ tevai Eṅkaḷukku pūrvvam Eṉṟum Iṭaiyile tāḻvu kāṭṭi vāṇikar naṭattukiṟomeṉkiṟa Itu tāḻvu paṭṭapaṭiyāle Itu tāḻvu paṭāmal naṭattumpaṭi cimāheśvararkkum tāṉattārkkum Icaiya nāṅkaḷ nāṭāka kuṭuppatāṉa Āṟṟūr nāṭṭu talaivāy Ittalaivāy nāṉkellaikku Uḷpaṭṭa naṉcey puṉcey karaip puṉceykku poṉvari Uḷḷiṭṭa Aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum
nāṭṭu viṉiyokamum Uḷpaṭa tirunāḷ tevaikku talaivāy kuṭiniṅkāt tevatāṉam Iṟaiyiliyākat tantom Āṟṟūr nāṭṭu nāṭṭavarom Ippaṭikku nāṉkellaiyum tiruccūlattāpaṉam paṇṇi kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoḷḷavum Ippaṭikku Āṟṟūr nāṭu kāṇippaṟṟu Aṟṟa kaṭamai koḷḷakkaṭavatu Ippaṭikku nāṭṭukkuc camaintu periya nāṭṭu ve(o)ḷāṉ Eḻuttu .
Digital edition of SII 28.278 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
